title: High Dose STZ Induction Protocol
author: Frank Brosius
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8iyhufw

steps.title: Prepare the buffers and solutions as described below
steps.instruction: Prepare the buffers and solutions as described below. Please note that the STZ- Na-Citrate solution should be prepared immediately prior to injection so as to avoid degradation of the STZ.

steps.title: Mice should be fasted prior to...
steps.instruction: Mice should be fasted prior to injection; four hours is usually sufficient.

steps.title: Place one mouse in the Isoflurane...
steps.instruction: Place one mouse in the Isoflurane drop jar, according to your local IACUC anesthesia standards.

steps.title: Remove mouse when breathing has slowed and animal is anesthetized
steps.instruction: Remove mouse when breathing has slowed and animal is anesthetized

steps.title: Inject appropriate amount of the STZ...
steps.instruction: Inject appropriate amount of the STZ solution IP so the final dosage is 150 mg/kg mouse

steps.title: Allow mouse to awaken and place back in cage
steps.instruction: Allow mouse to awaken and place back in cage

steps.title: Repeat procedure for each animal; keep...
steps.instruction: Repeat procedure for each animal; keep in mind that Isoflurane will need to be re- loaded after every third or fourth animal for proper anesthesia.

steps.title: Each mouse should be given one injection
steps.instruction: Each mouse should be given one injection.

steps.title: Supply mice with 10% sucrose water...
steps.instruction: Supply mice with 10% sucrose water overnight to avoid sudden hypoglycemia post-injection.

steps.title: Mice should be tested for sufficient...
steps.instruction: Mice should be tested for sufficient levels of hyperglycemia two days after injection and 4 weeks post-injection. Mice should be severely diabetic.
